```
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\nfrom mini_celery.models import JobState\n\n\nclass InvalidTransition(ValueError):\n    pass\n\n\nALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[JobState, set[JobState]] = {\n    JobState.QUEUED: {JobState.RUNNING, JobState.CANCELED, JobState.SUCCEEDED},\n    JobState.RUNNING: {\n        JobState.SUCCEEDED,\n        JobState.FAILED,\n        JobState.CANCELED,\n        JobState.DEAD,\n        JobState.QUEUED,\n    },\n    JobState.FAILED: {JobState.QUEUED},\n    JobState.DEAD: {JobState.QUEUED},\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED: set(),\n    JobState.CANCELED: set(),\n}\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass Transition:\n    old: JobState\n    new: JobState\n\n\ndef can_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> bool:\n    return new in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(old, set())\n\n\ndef assert_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> None:\n    if not can_transition(old, new):\n        raise InvalidTransition(f\"Invalid transition: {old} -> {new}\")\n"

          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport statistics\nimport time\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport httpx\nimport typer\n\nfrom mini_celery.config import get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.models import TERMINAL_STATES, JobState\n\napp = typer.Typer(no_args_is_help=True, help=\"mini-celery CLI\")\n\n\ndef _parse_payload(payload: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    try:\n        parsed = json.loads(payload)\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n        raise typer.BadParameter(f\"invalid JSON payload: {exc}\") from exc\n    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):\n        raise typer.BadParameter(\"payload must be a JSON object\")\n    return parsed\n\n\\REDACTED(\"submit\")\ndef submit(\n    task_type: str = typer.Argument(..., help=\"Task type\"),\n    payload: str = typer.Option(\"{}\", \"--payload\", help=\"JSON payload\"),\n    queue: str = typer.Option(\"default\", \"--queue\"),\n    max_attempts: int = typer.Option(3, \"--max-attempts\", min=1),\n    run_at: str | None = typer.Option(None, \"--run-at\", help=\"RFC3339 timestamp\"),\n    idempotency_key: str | None = typer.Option(None, \"--idempotency-key\"),\n) -> None:\n    settings = get_settings()\n    body: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"type\": task_type,\n        \"payload\": _parse_payload(payload),\n        \"queue\": queue,\n        \"max_attempts\": max_attempts,\n        \"idempotency_key\": idempotency_key,\n    }\n    if run_at:\n        body[\"run_at\"] = run_at\n\n    response = httpx.post(f\"{settings.api_url}/jobs\", json=body, timeout=10.0)\n    response.raise_for_status()\n    data = response.json()\n    typer.echo(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\n\n\\REDACTED(\"status\")\ndef status(job_id: str) -> None:\n    settings = get_settings()\n    response = httpx.get(f\"{settings.api_url}/jobs/{job_id}\", timeout=10.0)\n    response.raise_for_status()\n    typer.echo(json.dumps(response.json(), indent=2))\n\n\\REDACTED(\"list\")\ndef list_jobs(\n    state: str | None = typer.Option(None, \"--state\"),\n    task_type: str | None = typer.Option(None, \"--type\"),\n    limit: int = typer.Option(20, \"--limit\", min=1, max=100),\n    cursor: str | None = typer.Option(None, \"--cursor\"),\n) -> None:\n    settings = get_settings()\n    params = {\n        \"state\": state,\n        \"type\": task_type,\n        \"limit\": limit,\n        \"cursor\": cursor,\n    }\n    filtered = {k: v for k, v in params.items() if v is not None}\n    response = httpx.get(f\"{settings.api_url}/jobs\", params=filtered, timeout=10.0)\n    response.raise_for_status()\n    typer.echo(json.dumps(response.json(), indent=2))\n\n\\REDACTED(\"tail-events\")\ndef tail_events(\n    job_id: str,\n    poll_interval: float = typer.Option(1.0, \"--poll-interval\", min=0.1),\n) -> None:\n    settings = get_settings()\n    cursor_ts: str | None = None\n\n    while True:\n        params: dict[str, str | int] = {\"limit\": 200}\n        if cursor_ts:\n            params[\"cursor_ts\"] = cursor_ts\n\n        response = httpx.get(\n            f\"{settings.api_url}/jobs/{job_id}/events\",\n            params=params,\n            timeout=10.0,\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n        payload = response.json()\n        events = payload.get(\"items\", [])\n\n        for event in events:\n            typer.echo(json.dumps(event))\n            cursor_ts = event[\"ts\"]\n\n        status_resp = httpx.get(f\"{settings.api_url}/jobs/{job_id}\", timeout=10.0)\n        status_resp.raise_for_status()\n        state = status_resp.json()[\"state\"]\n        if JobState(state) in TERMINAL_STATES:\n            break\n\n        time.sleep(poll_interval)\n\n\nasync def _loadtest_impl(\n    *,\n    api_url: str,\n    total: int,\n    concurrency: int,\n    task_type: str,\n    payload: dict[str, Any],\n    max_attempts: int,\n) -> None:\n    submission_times: dict[str, float] = {}\n    completion_times: dict[str, float] = {}\n    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)\n\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=20.0) as client:\n\n        async def submit_one(index: int) -> None:\n            async with semaphore:\n                idempotency_key = f\"loadtest-{index}-{int(time.time() * 1000)}\"\n                request_body = {\n                    \"type\": task_type,\n                    \"payload\": payload,\n                    \"queue\": \"default\",\n                    \"max_attempts\": max_attempts,\n                    \"idempotency_key\": idempotency_key,\n                }\n                t0 = time.perf_counter()\n                response = await client.post(f\"{api_url}/jobs\", json=request_body)\n                response.raise_for_status()\n                job_id = response.json()[\"job_id\"]\n                submission_times[job_id] = t0\n\n        started = time.perf_counter()\n        await asyncio.gather(*[submit_one(i) for i in range(total)])\n        submitted_at = time.perf_counter()\n\n        pending = set(submission_times.keys())\n        while pending:\n            await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n            for job_id in list(pending):\n                response = await client.get(f\"{api_url}/jobs/{job_id}\")\n                response.raise_for_status()\n                state = JobState(response.json()[\"state\"])\n                if state in TERMINAL_STATES:\n                    completion_times[job_id] = time.perf_counter()\n                    pending.remove(job_id)\n\n        ended = time.perf_counter()\n\n    submit_throughput = total / max(submitted_at - started, 1e-9)\n    end_to_end_throughput = total / max(ended - started, 1e-9)\n    latencies = [completion_times[job_id] - submission_times[job_id] for job_id in submission_times]\n\n    def percentile(values: list[float], p: float) -> float:\n        if not values:\n            return 0.0\n        ordered = sorted(values)\n        idx = min(len(ordered) - 1, round((len(ordered) - 1) * p))\n        return ordered[idx]\n\n    report = {\n        \"total_jobs\": total,\n        \"submit_throughput_jobs_per_sec\": round(submit_throughput, 3),\n        \"end_to_end_throughput_jobs_per_sec\": round(end_to_end_throughput, 3),\n        \"latency_seconds\": {\n            \"min\": round(min(latencies), 4),\n            \"mean\": round(statistics.mean(latencies), 4),\n            \"p50\": round(percentile(latencies, 0.50), 4),\n            \"p95\": round(percentile(latencies, 0.95), 4),\n            \"max\": round(max(latencies), 4),\n        },\n    }\n    typer.echo(json.dumps(report, indent=2))\n\n\\REDACTED(\"loadtest\")\ndef loadtest(\n    total: int = typer.Option(100, \"--total\", min=1),\n    concurrency: int = typer.Option(10, \"--concurrency\", min=1),\n    task_type: str = typer.Option(\"sleep\", \"--task-type\"),\n    payload: str = typer.Option('{\"seconds\":0.05}', \"--payload\"),\n    max_attempts: int = typer.Option(3, \"--max-attempts\", min=1),\n) -> None:\n    settings = get_settings()\n    asyncio.run(\n        _loadtest_impl(\n            api_url=settings.api_url,\n            total=total,\n            concurrency=concurrency,\n            task_type=task_type,\n            payload=_parse_payload(payload),\n            max_attempts=max_attempts,\n        )\n    )\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    app()\n"
        },

    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "# mini-celery\n\nA local, production-style job queue + scheduler system in Python 3.12.\n\n- API: FastAPI + Uvicorn\n- Broker: Redis Streams + consumer groups\n- Persistence: SQLite + SQLAlchemy\n- Worker model: asyncio concurrency\n- Scheduler: delayed jobs + cron-like recurring jobs\n- Observability: JSON logs + Prometheus metrics\n\n## Architecture\n\n```text\n            +---------------------+\n            |      FastAPI API    |\n            |  /jobs, /healthz    |\n            |  /metrics            |\n            +----------+----------+\n                       |\n                       | durable writes\n                       v\n            +---------------------+\n            |   SQLite (jobs +    |\n            |   job_events +      |\n            |   idempotency +     |\n            |   schedule_ticks)   |\n            +---------------------+\n                       ^\n                       |\n                       |\n+----------------------+----------------------+\n|                                             |\n|                                   +---------+----------+\n|                                   |      Scheduler     |\n|                                   | delayed + recurring|\n|                                   +---------+----------+\n|                                             |\n|                                             | XADD\n|                                             v\n|                                   +--------------------+\n|                                   | Redis Streams      |\n|                                   | jobs:{queue}       |\n|                                   | jobs:dlq           |\n|                                   +----+----------+----+\n|                                        |          |\n|                              XREADGROUP|          |XPENDING/XCLAIM\n|                                        v          |\n|                                   +---------------+--+\n|                                   |      Worker      |\n|                                   | execute tasks    |\n|                                   +------------------+\n|\n+-----------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n## Reliability semantics\n\n### At-least-once delivery\n- Jobs are read with `XREADGROUP` from `jobs:{queue}`.\n- Worker acknowledges (`XACK`) **only after** final state transition is committed in SQLite.\n- If a worker crashes mid-run, message remains pending in PEL and can be reclaimed.\n\n### Visibility-timeout reclaim\n- Reclaim loop runs periodically.\n- Uses `XPENDING` (idle threshold) + `XCLAIM` to move stale messages to active consumer.\n- `jobs.lease_expires_at` lets the repository detect expired RUNNING attempts and safely requeue execution.\n\n### Durable state transitions\n- Every transition writes to `jobs` and appends one `job_events` row in the same DB transaction.\n- Transitions are validated by a strict state machine.\n\n### Idempotency\n- API de-dupes by `idempotency_key` for non-terminal jobs.\n- Worker checks durable `idempotency_locks` and short-circuits duplicate re-deliveries where a prior success already exists.\n\n## Data model\n\n### `jobs`\nRequired fields plus reliability fields:\n- `id`, `type`, `payload`, `state`, `created_at`, `updated_at`\n- `idempotency_key`, `attempts`, `max_attempts`, `next_run_at`, `schedule`\n- `last_error`, `result`\n- `queue`, `cancel_requested`, `lease_expires_at`, `last_stream_id`, `enqueued`, `version`\n\n### `job_events`\nAppend-only transition/event log:\n- `job_id`, `ts`, `old_state`, `new_state`, `message`, `metadata`\n\n### `idempotency_locks`\n- `key`, `owner_job_id`, `status`, `result`, `updated_at`\n\n### `schedule_ticks`\n- `schedule_id`, `planned_time`, `job_id`, `created_at`\n- unique `(schedule_id, planned_time)` for restart-safe dedupe\n\n## API\n\n- `POST /jobs`\n- `GET /jobs/{job_id}`\n- `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel`\n- `POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry`\n- `GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=`\n- `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events?cursor_ts=`\n- `GET /metrics`\n- `GET /healthz`\n\n## Built-in tasks\n\n- `sleep`\n- `http_get`\n- `cpu_burn`\n- `fail_then_succeed`\n- `pipeline_demo` (cooperative cancellation checkpoints between steps)\n\n## Metrics\n\n- `jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}`\n- `jobs_started_total{type}`\n- `jobs_finished_total{type,state}`\n- `job_duration_seconds{type}`\n- `queue_depth{queue}`\n- `worker_reclaims_total`\n- `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`\n\n## Quickstart\n\n### Requirements\n- Docker + Docker Compose\n- GNU make (optional; script fallback exists)\n\n### Start stack\n```bash\nmake up\n```\n\n### Stop stack\n```bash\nmake down\n```\n\n### Run lint\n```bash\nmake lint\n```\n\n### Run tests\n```bash\nmake test\n```\n\n### Run load test\n```bash\nmake loadtest\n```\n\n### Without make\n```bash\npython scripts/dev.py up\npython scripts/dev.py lint\npython scripts/dev.py test\npython scripts/dev.py down\n```\n\n## CLI usage\n\n```bash\nmini-celery submit sleep --payload '{\"seconds\":0.5}'\nmini-celery status <job_id>\nmini-celery list --state QUEUED --limit 50\nmini-celery tail-events <job_id>\nmini-celery loadtest --total 200 --concurrency 20 --task-type sleep --payload '{\"seconds\":0.02}'\n```\n\n## Scheduler config\n\n`config/schedules.yaml`:\n\n```yaml\nschedules:\n  - id: pipeline-minute\n    cron: \"* * * * *\"\n    queue: default\n    type: pipeline_demo\n    payload:\n      steps: [\"extract\", \"transform\", \"load\"]\n      step_delay: 0.1\n    max_attempts: 3\n    enabled: false\n```\n\n## Failure scenarios and handling\n\n### Worker crash mid-job\n- Job transitions to RUNNING before execution.\n- Message remains in PEL if worker dies before ack.\n- Another worker reclaims stale entry via `XPENDING` + `XCLAIM` and re-executes.\n\n### Redis restart\n- Stream/group state may reset if Redis is non-persistent.\n- Authoritative job state remains in SQLite (`enqueued=false` + `next_run_at`) so scheduler can re-enqueue due work.\n- At-least-once remains; duplicates are tolerated.\n\n### Scheduler restart\n- Recurring dedupe uses durable unique key `(schedule_id, planned_time)` in `schedule_ticks`.\n- Restart cannot enqueue the same planned tick twice beyond at-least-once semantics.\n\n## How to add a new task type\n\n1. Add a Pydantic payload model and async handler in `mini_celery/tasks/types.py`.\n2. Register it in `build_task_registry` with a `TaskSpec` and backoff policy.\n3. Add tests (payload validation + retry/cancel semantics if applicable).\n4. Submit with `POST /jobs` using the new `type`REDACTED Design tradeoffs\n\n- SQLite is simple and durable for local workloads; write concurrency is limited compared to Postgres.\n- Async worker concurrency is easier to reason about than multiprocess execution, but CPU-heavy tasks can block the event loop.\n- Immediate API enqueue is best-effort; durability is guaranteed by DB state even if broker publish temporarily fails.\n- At-least-once delivery intentionally permits duplicate execution for non-idempotent tasks.\n\n## Assumptions\n\n- All timestamps are UTC.\n- `retry` endpoint resets `attempts` to `0`, clears `last_error`, and requeues immediately.\n- Default queue is `default`.\n- Default `max_attempts = 3`.\n- Worker concurrency uses asyncio tasks.\n- Default backoff: base `1s`, multiplier `2.0`, cap `30s`, jitter ratio `0.2`.\n- Visibility timeout defaults to `30s`; reclaim interval defaults to `5s`.\n- `/jobs/{job_id}/events` is included to support CLI event tailing.\n- In environments without local `docker` or `make`, provided compose/make artifacts remain authoritative while `scripts/dev.py` offers command fallback.\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "4a13f4d269ba517cea30e788d54875710e8b4d51f0f8fef55f0d3d4dca5db7c0",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": "."
      },
      "4:VARINT": 1
    }
  ]
}
```
